meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve No Portfolio (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Use this beginner-friendly checklist to build passive income AI apps using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth. By leveraging AI-assisted development tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can ship production-ready micro-SaaS projects in hours. These projects serve as live proof of your engineering competency, attracting employers while generating passive income, effectively bridging the gap between student and professional developer.
content.intro:
The job market doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about what you've shipped. You are staring at a blank screen, feeling the crushing weight of 'no experience' while the industry demands senior-level output. It’s a paradox that keeps thousands of talented students stagnant, waiting for a permission slip that never comes.

But the rules have changed. With autonomous coding agents, you no longer need a decade of experience to build high-value software. You are about to bridge the gap between being a student and a founder. We are going to turn your lack of a portfolio into a competitive advantage by building a revenue-generating asset that proves your worth to the world.

steps.title: Define Your Micro-SaaS Niche
steps.description: Identify a small, painful problem in a specific community (like Discord or Reddit) that can be solved with a simple AI wrapper.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Brainstorm 5 micro-SaaS ideas that solve specific pain points for students, focusing on low-code implementation and high recurring value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude research --topic 'market analysis for student-focused micro-SaaS'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Structure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold your project architecture, ensuring modularity so you can scale features later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust directory structure for a Next.js application with a Supabase backend and Stripe integration for recurring payments.

steps.title: Implement Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your primary business logic, focusing on clean, maintainable, and well-documented code blocks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft the backend logic for a subscription-based AI content generator, including user authentication and usage rate limiting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement stripe checkout and auth middleware'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Configure an automated CI/CD pipeline so every push to your repository goes live immediately, demonstrating professional DevOps skills.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that automates testing and deployment to Vercel for a Next.js app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup github actions for automated deployment to vercel'

steps.title: Optimize for SEO and Visibility
steps.description: Ensure your project is discoverable by optimizing metadata and implementing social sharing previews.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write high-converting SEO meta tags and open-graph images for a landing page targeting professional developers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add dynamic meta tags and SEO optimization to the landing page'

pro_tips: Always build for the user, not the tech stack; a simple app that solves a real problem beats a complex app that solves nothing.
Use 'Claude Code' to write your tests first; this proves to employers you understand quality assurance.
Document your failures in your README; showing how you fixed a bug is more impressive than a perfect codebase.
Focus on 'Time to First Dollar'—keep the feature set minimal until you have at least one paying user.
Treat your GitHub commit history as a professional diary; make meaningful, descriptive commits that tell a story of your progress.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, write a 'Build Log' blog post detailing the challenges you solved. Share this on LinkedIn and Twitter to get your first users and build your personal brand.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the blueprint, and the strategy to stop being a student and start being a builder. Don't wait for a job offer to gain experience—create it yourself. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
